key_insight: A GNN trained only on finite Cayley graphs can transfer learned local algebraic structure to predict properties of truncated Cayley graphs from infinite finitely generated groups.
engineering_relevance: This suggests graph-based ML could help classify or probe group-theoretic structure from local connectivity patterns even when the target object is infinite and only partially observed.
method: Train and validate a GNN exclusively on finite complete and truncated Cayley graphs.
Test the same model without retraining on truncated Cayley graphs from unseen infinite groups.
Compare performance across families including free abelian groups, Heisenberg, infinite dihedral, free groups, and direct products.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports strong generalization on multiple unseen infinite-group families using only training on finite group Cayley graphs.
watch_item: Results rely on truncated graphs, so performance may depend on truncation depth and may not fully capture global group properties.
paper_title: From Finite Cayley Graphs to Growth of Infinite Groups
